name: PwnKit
description: Polkit pkexec local privilege escalation. A memory corruption vulnerability in polkit's pkexec allows any unprivileged user to escalate to root.
mitigation: Update polkit package: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install policykit-1

name: Baron Samedit
description: Sudo heap-based buffer overflow allows privilege escalation to root. Affects sudo versions 1.8.2 to 1.8.31p2 and 1.9.0 to 1.9.5p1.
mitigation: Update sudo: sudo apt-get install --only-upgrade sudo

name: Dirty Pipe
description: A flaw in the way the pipe buffer flags are handled in Linux kernel 5.8+ allows unprivileged users to overwrite read-only files and escalate privileges.

name: Dirty COW
description: Race condition in Linux kernel memory subsystem (copy-on-write) allows unprivileged local users to gain write access to read-only memory mappings.
mitigation: Update to kernel 4.8.3 or later; apply vendor patches

name: eBPF verifier vulnerability
description: The eBPF verifier in Linux kernel through 4.14.8 allows local unprivileged users to cause a denial of service or possibly gain privileges.

name: PTRACE_TRACEME privilege escalation
description: kernel/ptrace.c in Linux through 5.1.17 mishandles the initialization of tracee->parent, which allows local users to obtain root access.

name: eBPF verifier OOB read/write
description: Linux kernel 5.5.x through 5.5.7 has a heap-based OOB write in the eBPF verifier, allowing local privilege escalation.

name: Sequoia fs/seq_file
description: fs/seq_file.c in the Linux kernel through 5.13.3 allows local users to gain privileges via very large write operations to the /proc/<pid>/attr files.

name: nft_fwd_dup_netdev_offload heap OOB
description: An out-of-bounds write in the nft_fwd_dup_netdev_offload function in net/netfilter/nft_fwd_dup.c allows local users to escalate privileges.
mitigation: Update to kernel 5.17 or later; apply vendor patches

name: OverlayFS FUSE privilege escalation
description: A flaw was found in the Linux kernel with the OverlayFS subsystem when copying files from an unconfined FUSE file system. Unprivileged users may use this flaw for local privilege escalation.
mitigation: Update to kernel 6.2 or later; apply distribution patches

name: UFO packet corruption
description: Linux kernel since 2.6.x can cause memory corruption when a UFO packet is built and then fragmented, allowing local privilege escalation.

name: OverlayFS incorrect permission check
description: The overlayfs implementation in the linux kernel through 3.19.1 does not properly check permissions, allowing local users to gain privileges by leveraging user namespace usage.
mitigation: Update to kernel 4.0 or later; apply distribution patches
